to live, whether literally or figuratively; causatively, to revive

to walk (literally or figuratively); causatively, to carry (in various senses)
the right hand or side (leg, eye) of a person or other object (as the stronger and more dexterous); locally, the south

tightness (i.e. figuratively, trouble); transitively, a female rival:

tokens.range: gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a supe
David, the youngest son of Jesse
play upon it; to make music, accompanied by the voice; hence to celebrate in song and music
physically, to throw (a stone, an arrow) at or away; especially to revere or worship (with extended hands); intensively, to bemoan (by wringing the hands)
the heart; also used (figuratively) very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

flags.note: Dispute level 2: multiple significant translation options.
חֶסֶד — No English equivalent. Combines covenant loyalty, active kindness, and steadfast love. "Mercy" is too weak; "lovingkindness" is archaic; "steadfast love" misses the relational dimension.

flags.note: חֶסֶד — No English equivalent. Combines covenant loyalty, active kindness, and steadfast love. "Mercy" is too weak; "lovingkindness" is archaic; "steadfast love" misses the relational dimension.
יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.
עוֹלָם — "eternal" / "everlasting" / "age-long." Like Greek αἰώνιος, debate centers on whether this is absolute timelessness or the span of an era.

8.struct: contains a main verb; ⚑ theologically contested vocabulary

tokens.range: a day (as the warm hours), whether literal (from sunrise to sunset, or from one sunset to the next), or figurative (a space of time defined by an associated term), (often used adverb)
properly, a breathing creature, i.e. animal of (abstractly) vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense (bodily or mental)
strength in various applications (force, security, majesty, praise)
properly, to eye or (generally) to heed, i.e. pay attention; by implication, to respond; by extension to begin to speak; specifically to sing, shout, testify, announce
to call out to (i.e. properly, address by name, but used in a wide variety of applications)
to urge severely, i.e. (figuratively) importune, embolden, capture, act insolently

flags.note: נֶפֶשׁ — "soul" (Greek-influenced: non-material self) vs. "life" (the whole living person) vs. "self" (the person as subject). Hebrew nefesh is embodied, not a separate immaterial substance.
